thesis: Three lenses on one signed corpus. Scry watches who is attacking; Sigil watches who can be trusted; Tracker watches who is paying whom. The join across the three is the thing no siloed incumbent can compute — and it is what an autonomous agent needs to answer the only question that matters when it transacts with a new endpoint: is this real?

products.name: Scry
products.summary: Signed observations of hostile network actors with actor-class enrichment and threat-feed overlap.

key_endpoints.purpose: Per-IP reputation lookup with actor classification

products.name: Sigil
products.summary: Programmatic-advertising supply graph. Publishers, SSPs, DSPs, seats, OpenRTB SupplyChain, entity trust scoring.

key_endpoints.purpose: Resolve a domain to its sell-side presence in the supply graph
Resolve an entity slug to its observed surface across the supply graph
Verify a SupplyChain path with a weighted-mean verdict
Mint an ATAP capability token for the calling agent

products.name: Tracker
products.summary: Surveillance-economy graph. Tracker entities, the resellers they buy from, the publishers they reach.

key_endpoints.purpose: Resolve a tracker host to its operating entity and reseller relationships

products.name: Cross-lens verify
products.summary: The A2 join. Service-layer fusion of Scry × Sigil × Tracker for one node key. Returns the fused verdict, per-lens blocks, a 5-minute signed sigil_token, and an optional ATAP witness event.

key_endpoints.purpose: Fused cross-lens verdict for an IP, domain, ASN, or entity_slug. Optional ait field wraps the verdict in an ATAP cross_lens:verified witness event.

mcp_servers.summary: Every Data API operation surfaced as an MCP tool. Tracker, Sigil, and cross_lens_verify in one surface.
Scry-only MCP. Free IPv4 lookups against the attacker-observation corpus. No auth.
Sigil-scoped MCP. Curated tool surface. Includes cross_lens_verify with identical semantics to the data-api surface.

cross_product_tools.name: cross_lens_verify
cross_product_tools.summary: Exposed on both Sigil and Data API MCP servers. The right answer for 'is this real?' should not depend on which MCP an agent landed at.

free_tier.scope: All identifier resolution is free, forever. Radar sample is public.

paid_tier.summary: Stripe for humans; x402 USDC micropayments for agents. Per-endpoint pricing in OpenAPI x-tunnelmind-pricing.

agent_payments.summary: x402 is the agent-native rail. Stripe is the human rail. Demo endpoint accepts mode='demo' HMAC payments (no USDC required) for client-validation smoke. Real-mode mainnet USDC verification pending wallet provisioning + facilitator credentials. Pricing per endpoint lives in the OpenAPI x-tunnelmind-pricing extension.

byom_analyst_config.summary: BYOM (bring-your-own-model) config bundle that turns any LLM into a TunnelMind analyst. System prompt + tool subset + response schema + attestation-tier vocabulary. Customer brings the LLM and the tokens; TunnelMind earns on data API calls.
byom_analyst_config.receipt_wrap: Add ?receipt=true to wrap the response in a signed Receipt v1.0 envelope
byom_analyst_config.verification: Inline bundle_signature is Ed25519 over JCS(bundle minus bundle_signature). Verify against /.well-known/receipt-signing-key.json.

principles.summary: Every observation is Ed25519-signed by the sensor that made it.
The radar shows a live sample to anyone, no account. Identifiers resolve for free.
Every observed actor gets a permanent handle (OAI).
We make the watchers legible. We do not fabricate data.
Anything that analyzes a person's traffic runs on their machine.
